propositions: Retired but still-set env vars and TOML keys are silently ignored or defaulted, redirecting the store or weakening policy without warning.
Introduce a RemovedInput tombstone registry that maps a retired input to old name, replacement, removed-in version, and error/warn behavior, applied before defaults are chosen.
Error-class tombstones raise RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it is not swallowed by tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError or MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.
Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks must run at the top of load_config() before the result cache lookup so they fire on warm-cache MCP hits.
TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR check is placed inside tracker_dir_override() to cover bypassing readers that don't flow through load_config().
Add a rebar config validate/doctor command using scan_tombstones() to report all matches without raising, plus a CI golden-file guard failing if a key is removed without a tombstone.
